title: Handle Emergency Flights
description: The system shall immediately allocate the next available slot to an incoming emergency flight and re-queue all other previously queued flights in order with adjusted slots.
rationale: To ensure rapid response to critical situations, prioritizing emergency flights above all others is essential for public safety.
business_value: Improves response time during emergencies, potentially saving lives by ensuring quick handling of critical flights.

source.description: As a air traffic controller I need the system to handle emergency flights by immediately allocating the next available slot and re-queuing all other affected flights to make room for the emergency so that ensuring rapid response to critical situations.

acceptance_criteria: An incoming emergency flight is detected.
The system allocates the next available slot.
The emergency flight is inserted into that slot.
All other previously queued flights are re-queued in order with adjusted slots.
A queue of non-emergency flights exists.
An emergency flight is allocated a slot.
No non-emergency flight loses its original order.
The emergency flight is placed at the front of the queue (in the next available slot).
preconditions: The system has a queue of scheduled flights with assigned slots.
Emergency flights are flagged and prioritized above all others.
postconditions: The emergency flight is handled immediately by allocating the next available slot.
All other previously queued flights are re-queued in order with adjusted slots.
No non-emergency flight loses its original order.
The emergency flight is placed at the front of the queue.
functional_constraints: Slot allocation for emergencies does not require manual confirmation—it happens automatically.
edge_cases: What happens if there are no available slots for emergency flights?
How does the system handle multiple emergencies simultaneously?
What if a non-emergency flight is re-queued but its adjusted slot causes delays?
dependencies: A queue management system to store and manage scheduled flights.
An emergency detection mechanism to flag incoming emergency flights.

interfaces.name: Flight Queue API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the Flight Queue API to retrieve, update, and re-queue flight slots.

interfaces.name: Flight Schedule Database
interfaces.description: The requirement uses the Flight Schedule Database to store and manage scheduled flights and their slots.

test_cases.description: Test allocation of next available slot for an emergency flight.
test_cases.expected_result: The system inserts the emergency flight into the next available slot and re-queues all other flights in order with adjusted slots.

test_cases.description: Test handling of multiple emergencies simultaneously.
test_cases.expected_result: The system allocates slots to each emergency flight in the order they were detected and re-queues other flights accordingly.
